name: alert-dialog
description: A confirm modal: an interruptive prompt requiring a response (delete this? discard?). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 3), the role=alertdialog specialization of the dialog wire: open-state + title/description/labels + the destructive flag live in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the destructive action + cancel are real l:click buttons, not slots. Renders the dialog wire's overlay/panel structure directly (no nested live instance, no <lv-dialog> island). WAI-ARIA APG alertdialog (role=alertdialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); the backdrop does NOT dismiss (only the explicit buttons do); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.
docs: Register AlertDialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.alert-dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` from a trigger to show the prompt, `confirm` (the destructive/primary action) or `cancel` to close. Do the real destructive work server-side by overriding `confirm()` (with authz) before it closes, or read the `confirmed` wire flag on the host after the round-trip. An alertdialog is interruptive: the backdrop does NOT dismiss it, only the explicit buttons do. Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-alert-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the `cancel` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=cancel button, which is also the initial-focus element via data-lv-autofocus -- the focus-on-cancel default for an interruptive prompt), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/alert-dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).
